Home.title: Welcome to
Home.subtitle: Ashley Leon
Home.hero-title-two: and live workshops for returning to what's
Home.hero-tagline: Writing, workbooks, and live workshops for returning to what is sacred without self-abandonment.
Home.hero-description: For people rebuilding faith, identity, and belonging after inherited belief no longer fits. Do the work on your own, or do it in a room with others who left too.
Home.intro-lead: Ashley Leon is a digital home for reflection, spiritual integration, and honest language.
Home.paragraph-description: It exists for those navigating faith deconstruction, queerness, cultural identity, and the slow work of becoming whole.
Home.paragraph-description-two: Here you'll find weekly reflections, honest language, and grounded spirituality—for people rebuilding reverence toward themselves, the divine, and their humanity.
Home.find-blurb: Everything we've been tending — take what your moment needs.
Home.find-item-1-desc: Essays to think, question, and come back to ground.
Home.find-item-2-desc: Small digital books to practice at your own pace.
Home.find-item-3-desc: Phrases and voices to accompany you in the everyday.
Home.find-item-4: Resources for rebuilding faith beyond dogma
Home.find-item-4-desc: Language and prayer beyond inherited religion.
Home.find-item-5-desc: Live gatherings for those who want to walk accompanied.
Home.who-title: If you are rebuilding language, faith, or belonging, there is room here to breathe.
Home.who-quote-1: I do not want to lose the sacred; I want to find it without losing myself.
Home.who-quote-2: I need gentle tools for learning how to hear my own voice again.
Home.who-quote-3: I want to walk with people who understand this process.
Home.manifesto-title: An edition for those returning to themselves, slowly.
Home.manifesto-pillar-1-desc: Stories, tools, and exercises for your process of personal reconstruction.
Home.manifesto-pillar-2-desc: No dogma, no judgment. Only deep content, real integration, and space to be yourself.
Home.manifesto-pillar-3-desc: You are not alone. Here you find a safe space to return to yourself, in community.
Home.fresh-title-one: New reflections. New language. Same intention.
Home.paragraph-fresh: New reflections on faith, identity, healing, and return.
Home.book-blurb-start: A guided workbook to help you
Home.book-blurb-end: for your own becoming—away from inherited fear, and back toward the sacred in you.
Home.book-empty: Our next edition is on its way. Sign up below to be the first to know.
Home.circle-title: When you're ready to go deeper: The Rebuilding Reverence Circle.
Home.circle-description: A small, live 4-week group experience capped at 15 people, where we move through the journey together with Ashley in the room.
Home.community-description: A grounded, affirming online community for reflection, honest conversation, and people walking the same in-between road.
Home.writing-empty: The next writings are on the way.
Home.sidebar-cta-desc: Stories, affirmations, and reflections—straight to your inbox, once a week.

metadata.title: About Ashley Leon | Writer, Coach & Workshop Facilitator on Faith & Identity
metadata.description: Ashley Leon is a writer, workshop facilitator, and certified holistic mind-body coach working at the intersection of faith deconstruction, queer spirituality, and emotional healing.

About.title: I'm Ashley.
About.paragraph-1: I'm a writer, workshop facilitator, and certified holistic mind-body coach. My life has been shaped in the in-between — the space between the faith I was handed and the faith I'm still learning to trust. I lived deeply inside religion, and then I learned how to leave it without losing reverence.
About.paragraph-2: I'm Cuban-Colombian, queer, first-generation, a former full-time missionary, and a seminary student who left with more questions than answers. I lived in the extremes for a long time. Now, I choose the gray.
About.paragraph-3: My work lives at the intersection of faith deconstruction, queer spirituality, and emotional healing — spirituality beyond dogma, faith without fear, integration instead of erasure.
About.paragraph-4: I'm not here to tell you what to believe. I'm here to hold space, alongside my wife, for the slow, honest work of return.

metadata.title: Heritage Recipes — Venezuelan & Latin American Cooking | Ashley Leon
metadata.description: Venezuelan and Latin American heritage recipes. Traditional dishes passed down and reimagined with love, memory, and cultural pride.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: At Ashley Leon, we value your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices you have regarding your data when you visit our site or use our services.

informationCollected.title: 1. Information we collect
informationCollected.body:
We may collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email address, and message content when you contact us or subscribe to our newsletter.

We may also collect basic technical data such as browser type, visited pages, and date/time of access to improve the site experience.

howWeUse.title: 2. How we use your information
howWeUse.body: We use your information to: (a) respond to inquiries, (b) send communications you requested, (c) process purchases or digital deliveries when applicable, and (d) improve the site's content, performance, and security.

sharing.title: 3. Data sharing
sharing.body: We do not sell your personal information. We may share data with service providers that help us operate the site (for example payment, hosting, analytics, or email services) under confidentiality obligations and only to provide those services.

cookies.title: 4. Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body: We use essential technologies for site functionality and, only with your permission, Google Analytics 4 to understand visits, article engagement, and interactions with resource links. Google Analytics may set the first-party _ga cookie and process information such as visited pages, general device and browser details, session information, and approximate location. We do not send form responses, email addresses, or payment details to Analytics. You can accept, decline, or later change analytics consent using the Cookie settings control on the site.

security.title: 5. Information security
security.body: We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational safeguards to protect your information. However, no method of transmission or storage is 100% secure, so we cannot guarantee absolute security.

rights.title: 6. Your rights and choices
rights.body: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights to request access, correction, update, or deletion of your personal data, and to withdraw consent where applicable. To exercise these rights, contact us using the email listed in this policy.

children.title: 7. Children's privacy
children.body: This site is not intentionally directed to children under the age defined by applicable law. If you believe a minor has provided personal data to us, contact us so we can review and take appropriate action.

intlTransfers.title: 8. International transfers
intlTransfers.body: Your data may be processed in countries other than your own, where data protection laws may differ. Where appropriate, we implement reasonable safeguards to protect transferred information.

changes.title: 9. Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time to reflect legal, technical, or operational changes. We will post the current version on this page and indicate the latest update date.

contact.title: 10. Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, you can contact us at:

Terms.title: Terms and Conditions
Terms.intro: By accessing and using this website, you agree to these Terms and Conditions. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
acceptance.body: These terms govern your use of the site and services offered by Ashley Leon. By browsing, signing up, subscribing, or purchasing, you confirm that you have read and accepted these terms.

useOfSite.title: 2. Permitted use of the site
useOfSite.body: You agree to use the site lawfully and respectfully. You must not engage in activities that impact the site's security, availability, or operation, or attempt unauthorized access to systems or data.

intellectualProperty.title: 3. Intellectual property
intellectualProperty.body: Unless otherwise stated, site content (text, images, brand assets, design, and resources) belongs to Ashley Leon or their respective owners. You may not copy, distribute, or commercially exploit this content without prior written authorization.

userConduct.title: 4. User conduct
userConduct.body: You may not post or send unlawful, offensive, defamatory, fraudulent content, or content that infringes third-party rights. We reserve the right to restrict access or remove content that violates these terms.

purchases.title: 5. Purchases and payments
purchases.body: When making purchases, you agree to provide accurate and complete information. Payments are processed through third-party providers. Delivery times, access, and use of digital products may be subject to specific conditions shown during checkout.

thirdParty.title: 6. Third-party links and services
thirdParty.body: The site may include links to third-party platforms or services. We do not control those sites and are not responsible for their content, policies, or practices. We recommend reviewing their terms and policies before use.

disclaimers.title: 7. Disclaimers
disclaimers.body: Site content is provided for general informational and educational purposes. It does not constitute legal, medical, psychological, financial, or other professional advice.

liability.title: 8. Limitation of liability
liability.body: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Ashley Leon is not liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from use of, or inability to use, the site.

indemnification.title: 9. Indemnification
indemnification.body: You agree to defend and indemnify Ashley Leon against claims, losses, or expenses arising from your breach of these terms or misuse of the site.

termination.title: 10. Termination
termination.body: We may suspend or terminate access to the site, in whole or in part, if we detect misuse, breach of these terms, or for justified operational reasons.

governingLaw.title: 11. Governing law
governingLaw.body: These terms are interpreted under the applicable law of the jurisdiction corresponding to the site owner, unless mandatory legal provisions state otherwise.

changes.title: 12. Changes to the terms
changes.body: We may update these terms when necessary. The updated version will be published on this page with its effective date. Continued use of the site means acceptance of the changes.

contact.title: 13. Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about these Terms and Conditions, you can contact us at:

RebuildingReverence.metadata-description: A 30-day return to wonder after the ruins of religion. A slow, body-first practice to rebuild reverence without self-abandonment.

hero.title: REBUILDING REVERENCE
hero.subtitle: A 30-day return to wonder after the ruins of religion
hero.tagline: You're not crazy. You're not backsliding. You're not orphaned. You're waking up.

if-you.heading: If any of this is you…
items: You've whispered a prayer and wondered if anyone was still listening.
You left a church and felt like you left God somewhere in the parking lot.
You were told your questions were dangerous, your body was a problem, your softness was sin.
You served more, hid more, sacrificed more — and called it belonging.
You've been blaming the devil for every hard thing, and you're so tired of living on guard.
if-you.closing: Then you already know the truth no one in your old pew would say out loud:
if-you.truth: You didn't lose your faith. You outgrew the cage it came in.

different-way.heading: A different way back
different-way.paragraph1: Rebuilding Reverence is for the ones who walked away — not from God, but from the version of God that couldn't hold your questions, your culture, or your full humanity.
different-way.paragraph2: It's a 30-day path back to a faith that doesn't require self-abandonment.
different-way.paragraph3: Not a quick fix. Not a spiritual sugar rush. A slow, honest, body-first practice that meets you exactly where you are — tender, unsure, still aching, still here.

each-day.heading: What each day holds
each-day.intro: Ten minutes. No performance. No pew required.

anchors.name: Arrival
anchors.desc: a breath to bring you into your body

anchors.name: Anchor
anchors.desc: one true thing to carry through your day

anchors.name: Reflect
anchors.desc: a story that finds you where you live

anchors.name: Respond
anchors.desc: a prompt that moves something loose

each-day.closing: You don't have to go in order. You don't have to rush. This isn't a test. It's a return.

items.title: The 30-Day Journey
items.desc: Four weeks: Questioning the Old Blueprint, Reclaiming Trust, Rewriting What's True, Reconstructing from the Inside Out.

items.title: Guided Audio Companion
items.desc: Every Arrival and key reflection, in my voice, so you can close your eyes and let yourself be led instead of leading yourself.

items.title: The Circle
items.desc: A community of people walking the same road, so you never have to do the leaving and the returning alone.

items.title: The Release Toolkit
items.desc: The closing prayer, the "When I Feel Afraid" integration practice, and the voices that walked me home.

who-for.heading: Who this is for
who-for.paragraph1: The deconstructing. The questioning. The queer and called. The ones grieving a church that wasn't safe — and hungry for a sacred that is.
who-for.paragraph2: If you're done being afraid of what you feel, you're already home here.

promise.heading: My promise to you
promise.paragraph1: Move through all 30 days. If nothing shifts, email me and I'll refund you — no scripture required.
promise.paragraph2: I'd rather you trust the process than feel trapped by a purchase. That's the whole point.

behind.heading: Who's behind this
behind.paragraph1: I'm Ashley. I didn't grow up religious — but when I found evangelical Christianity, I gave it everything. For a season, it saved me. Then it began to cost me things I didn't know I was allowed to keep: my intuition, my voice, my joy.
behind.paragraph2: Eventually I had to walk away. Not from God — from the version of God that couldn't hold all of me. That's where the real journey began.
behind.paragraph3: I'm a certified holistic mind-body coach with a background in psychology, and these pages hold what took me years to unravel — so your own path home can feel a little less lonely.

items.a: No. This isn't about throwing it all away — it's about sifting, not scorning. There's beauty, grief, poetry, and truth in what you inherited. We hold the good without denying the pain.

items.q: What if I'm still in church?
items.a: Welcome. This isn't a push to leave anything. It's a space to be honest about what you carry, wherever you stand.
That's okay. Speak your answers aloud. Take them on a walk. Use the audio and let your body respond. Whatever keeps you honest, start there.

items.q: How much time does it take?
items.a: About ten minutes a day. You're allowed to pause, repeat a day, or stay in a question longer than expected.
Yes. All of you belongs here — your culture, your body, your sexuality, your questions. Reverence should never require hiding.

closing.heading: One last thing
closing.paragraph1: The altar was never just wood and gold. After all the leaving and returning, you'll find something unshakeable: the Divine within.

metadata.title: Online Faith Deconstruction Community | The In-Between
metadata.description: A grounded, affirming online community for people rebuilding faith, spirituality, identity, and belonging after deconstruction.

Community.title: Somewhere to keep returning.
Community.lead: A grounded, affirming space for rebuilding faith, identity, and belonging after deconstruction.
Community.paragraph: The In-Between is our online community: the place where this work stays alive between workbooks and circles. A grounded, affirming space for honest reflection, real conversation, and belonging without conditions.
Community.inside-1: Ongoing reflections and prompts to keep the work alive between workbooks and circles.
Community.inside-2: Honest conversation — no performance, no pressure to have it figured out.
Community.inside-3: A place to return to yourself in company.
Community.footnote: Free to join. The In-Between lives on a private, members-only platform.

Circle.metadata-title: Online Faith Deconstruction Group | Rebuilding Reverence Circle
Circle.metadata-description: A live four-week online group for people rebuilding faith, identity, trust, and belonging after religious deconstruction.
Circle.earlyRateNote: Early cohort rate. Regular price ${regular}. Founding members receive the reduced rate in exchange for feedback that shapes future circles.

hero.title: The Rebuilding Reverence Circle
hero.subtitle: A 4-week live group experience for those done rebuilding faith alone

lines: Some things you can't journal your way out of alone.
People who don't need you to explain why you left — because they left too.

what-circle-is.heading: What the Circle is
what-circle-is.paragraph1: The Rebuilding Reverence Circle is a small, intimate 4-week live group experience where we move through the journey together — with Ashley in the room.
what-circle-is.paragraph2: Not a course. Not a webinar. Not a lecture.
what-circle-is.paragraph3: A circle. Where everyone gets to speak. Where your story belongs. Where the work actually lands because someone is witnessing it with you.
what-circle-is.paragraph4: The Circle runs in cohorts — small, intentional groups that begin together and move through the journey together. A new cohort opens only when a full circle of 15 is complete. This is not a program you can drop into at any time. When a cohort is full, it's full — and the next one opens when we're ready to begin again.

items.title: 4 Live Weekly Sessions with Ashley
items.desc: Every week we gather on Zoom for 60–75 minutes. Ashley opens the space, teaches, holds what comes up, and closes with a practice you carry into the week. Sessions are recorded so you never miss one.

items.title: A Capped Circle of 15 People Maximum
items.desc: Small by design. Big enough to feel a community. Small enough that you are never invisible. This is not a webinar with hundreds of strangers. Everyone in this room is here on purpose.

items.title: The Full Rebuilding Reverence Journey
items.desc: Your 30-day journal and daily guided meditations are included — the live sessions are structured to move alongside them, week by week. No separate purchase needed.

items.title: Mid-Week Voice Notes from Ashley
items.desc: Between sessions, a short 3–5 minute voice note lands in the community — a grounding, a teaching, a reminder that you're not walking this alone. Just enough to keep the practice alive between sessions.

items.title: Private Circle Community
items.desc: A dedicated thread inside the Rebuilding Reverence community just for circle members. For the 3am thoughts. The breakthroughs. The things you need to say before next week's session.

items.title: Optional: 1:1 Integration Call After Week 4
items.desc: If you want to close the circle with a private 45-minute conversation with Ashley — to integrate what came up, discern what's next, and take the next step with support — this option is available to circle members after the final session.

weeks.name: Week 1 — Questioning the Old Blueprint
weeks.desc: We begin with honesty. What were we handed? What did it cost us? What do we want to sift from what we want to release? You'll leave this session with language for things you've been carrying without words.

weeks.name: Week 2 — Reclaiming Trust
weeks.desc: The room gets warmer here. We've been journaling for a week. We go deeper — into the leaders who failed us, the God we're learning to trust again, the inner knowing we were told not to trust. This session does something.

weeks.name: Week 3 — Rewriting What's True
weeks.desc: This is where it tends to break open. We dismantle inherited beliefs about sin, authority, the Bible, our bodies. We replace them not with new dogma but with deeper truth. Ashley's coaching background holds the room here.

weeks.name: Week 4 — Reconstructing from the Inside Out
weeks.desc: The closing session. A ritual of return. Where do we carry this? What does belonging look like now? What do we bless and release? This session ends with a blessing over every person in the circle.

who-for.heading: Who this is for
who-for.paragraph1: You've been in a deconstruction season — maybe for months, maybe for years.
who-for.paragraph2: You've read the books. You've listened to the podcasts. You've journaled alone.
who-for.paragraph3: But something is still unfinished. And you're starting to suspect that finishing it requires other people.
who-for.paragraph4: This circle is for the exvangelical. The questioning. The queer and called. The ones who still light candles and don't know why. The ones whose faith got smaller before it got real.
who-for.belong: If that's you — you belong here.

ashley-promise.heading: Ashley's promise
ashley-promise.paragraph1: I created Rebuilding Reverence during my own unraveling. I'm a certified holistic mind-body coach, a writer, a former full-time missionary, a seminary student who left with more questions than answers.
ashley-promise.paragraph2: I'm Cuban-Colombian. I'm queer. I'm first-gen. I've lived in extremes and I chose the gray.
ashley-promise.paragraph3: I'm not going to tell you what to believe. I'm going to hold space for you to find out.

enrollment.heading: How Enrollment Works
enrollment.paragraph1: The Circle is cohort-based — meaning we begin together, move through the four weeks together, and close together.
enrollment.paragraph2: A new cohort opens once we reach a full circle of 15 members. This is not a rolling program. We don't start with 5 people and figure it out — we wait until the room is full, because the room is the point.
enrollment.intro: Here's what happens when you reserve your spot:
steps: You'll receive a confirmation with everything you need to prepare.
Once the cohort reaches 15, your confirmed start date is sent to the full group.
If this cohort fills before you join, you are automatically added to the waitlist for the next round — no action needed on your part.
enrollment.note: The Circle opens only a few times a year. If you feel the pull, don't wait on it.

cohort-status.heading: Current Cohort Status
cohort-status.spotsNote: This number updates as spots are reserved. When it hits zero, enrollment closes and the waitlist opens.

items.q: Do I need to have bought the PDF first?
items.a: No — the journal and meditations are included in your circle registration. You arrive with everything you need.

items.q: What if I can't make a live session?
items.a: All sessions are recorded and shared with circle members within 24 hours. You won't fall behind.
Sessions are scheduled to work for the majority of registered members. Your confirmed time is shared once the cohort is full.

items.q: How long until we begin once I sign up?
items.a: We begin when the circle of 15 is complete. Depending on where enrollment stands, that could be days or a few weeks. You'll be notified the moment your start date is confirmed.

No. Ashley is a certified holistic mind-body coach, not a licensed therapist. The circle is a coaching and community space. If you are in active crisis, please seek licensed mental health support alongside this work.
Fully. All of you belongs here — your culture, your body, your sexuality, your questions.

items.q: What if it's not what I expected?
items.a: Reach out within the first week and we'll make it right.

closing.heading: One more thing
closing.paragraph1: The altar was never just wood and gold.
closing.paragraph3: You don't have to rebuild this alone.
closing.finalLine: 15 people. One circle. A few times a year.
